mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate rises above 2.0% and continues climbing, the cost of capital increases, reducing the present value of future corporate earnings. This produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days as equity valuations compress in response to tighter monetary conditions.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 2.33% as of 2022-08-01 in tightening cycle
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 55% of instances where FEDFUNDS > 2.0% and rising, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window during tightening regimes.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the unemployment rate below 4.0% signals tight labor market conditions that increase wage pressures and corporate cost inflation, reducing profit margins and equity valuations over a 60-day forward horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 3.6% as of 2022-08-01, below 4.0% threshold
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with labor market tightness
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are not significantly negative (mean return > 0% with t-test p > 0.10) when UNRATE < 4.0%, or Rank IC < 0.025 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp month-over-month decline in retail sales (RSAFS) signals weakening consumer demand and economic growth deceleration. This triggers negative equity returns over 20 trading days as investors reprrice growth expectations downward.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of instances following RSAFS declines > 2.0%, or Rank IC < 0.015 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) and the federal funds rate jointly determine equity returns: when INDPRO growth slows below 1.0% annualized while FEDFUNDS remains elevated above 2.0%, the combination signals stagflation risk, producing negative 60-day forward returns as investors flee equities.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 104.55 and FEDFUNDS at 2.33% as of 2022-08-01
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns are positive in > 50% of stagflation-signal instances, or the interaction term (INDPRO × FEDFUNDS) produces Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) and retail sales (RSAFS) jointly capture demand momentum: when both decline simultaneously by > 1.5% month-over-month, it signals broad-based demand weakness that produces negative 20-day equity returns as forward growth expectations compress.
candidate_trigger: HOUST declines > 1.5% AND RSAFS declines > 1.5% month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1446K and RSAFS at 683291M as of 2022-08-01
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 55% of dual-decline instances, or the joint signal produces Rank IC < 0.018 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment (PAYEMS) growth decelerates below 100K new jobs per month while the unemployment rate remains stable or rises, signaling labor market deterioration that precedes broader economic weakness and produces negative 5-day equity returns as forward guidance is revised downward.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS monthly change < 100K AND UNRATE rises > 0.1pp month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 152744K and UNRATE at 3.6% as of 2022-08-01
regime_hint: labor market deterioration or early-cycle slowdown
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 60% of labor-deterioration instances, or the interaction signal produces Rank IC < 0.01 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation (CPIAUCSL) accelerates above 8.0% year-over-year while the federal funds rate lags inflation by > 5.5 percentage points, signaling real rates remain negative and monetary policy is insufficiently restrictive. This produces positive 60-day equity returns as investors expect eventual policy capitulation and rate cuts.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 295.62 and FEDFUNDS at 2.33% as of 2022-08-01
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in > 50% of policy-lag instances, or the interaction term produces Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) decelerates to below 1.0% annualized while housing starts (HOUST) remain above 1.3M units annualized, signaling demand resilience despite growth slowdown. This produces positive 20-day equity returns as the market reprices recession risk downward and recognizes housing strength as a growth stabilizer.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 growth < 1.0% annualized AND HOUST > 1.3M units annualized
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 19699.465B and HOUST at 1446K as of 2022-08-01
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in > 55% of resilience-signal instances, or the interaction term produces Rank IC < 0.015 over any 2-year rolling window.
